title: Mutation Authority Envelope
description: The typed envelope (execution-plan.md section 6.4, ADR-005) every protected writer must accept or resolve via an opaque handle. No handler, hook, script, adapter, or library may call the underlying writer directly. Carries the 14 execution-plan section 6.4 fields plus an optional terminal `result` (commit outcome / structured denial), both bound to contracts/reason-code-registry.json. Runtime-neutral. Real content P330.

sicFingerprint.description: Exact approved SIC body fingerprint (§6.4 field 1).

dtcFingerprint.description: Exact approved DTC body fingerprint (§6.4 field 2).

userDecisionEvidence.description: Independently verified user-decision evidence (§6.4 field 3) — not tool success, not a free-text reference.

consumerOntology.description: Consumer Ontology identity and version (§6.4 field 4).

targetIdentities.description: Target primitive or object identities (§6.4 field 5).

writeScope.description: Allowed write scope (§6.4 field 6, scope half).

submissionCriteriaResult.description: Submission-criteria result, kept separate from the permission decision (§6.4 field 8). Mandatory, not an optional caller-supplied list that silently passes when omitted (closes P220 section 8.4's vacuous-when-omitted gap).

issuingDecision.description: Issuing decision reference (§6.4 field 9, decision half).

expectedPriorState.description: Expected prior state or concurrency token (§6.4 field 13).

outcome.description: Commit result or structured-failure outcome, stamped once the single commit gate resolves this envelope.

reasonCode.description: Stable denial/outcome reason code (§6.4 field 14), from contracts/reason-code-registry.json.

result.description: Absent while the envelope is still pending resolution; present and required-complete once the gate has resolved it, either as a commit result or a structured denial/error.
